F01.name: deck_manager
F01.desc: Handles shuffling 54 cards, dealing 17 cards to each player, assigning 3 landlord cards, and resetting for new games

F03.name: turn_controller
F03.desc: Manages counter-clockwise turn order, tracks whose turn it is, enforces pass logic, and detects round resets

F05.name: player_input
F05.desc: Handles mouse click selection/deselection of hand cards, and play/pass/hint button clicks

F07.name: win_detector
F07.desc: Checks after each play whether any player's hand is empty, determines win/loss result, triggers game-over state

F08.name: ui_renderer
F08.desc: Renders player hand area, play area, AI card counts, control buttons, status bar, highlight states, and result dialog

F02_3.name: bonus_card_dealer
F02_3.desc: Reveals the 3 reserved bonus cards and adds them to the landlord's hand, adjusting the landlord's card count and hand composition

F02_4.name: fallback_random_assigner
F02_4.desc: Handles edge cases where no player bids for landlord, applying fallback logic such as random assignment, forced selection, or triggering a redeal

F02_1_1.name: bid_turn_sequencer
F02_1_1.desc: Controls the order and progression of turns in the bidding round, determining which player is next to bid, handling pass-throughs, and detecting when the bidding round has concluded

F02_1_2.name: bid_action_validator
F02_1_2.desc: Validates whether a player's bid action (call, grab, pass) is legal given the current bidding state, enforcing rules such as bid escalation requirements and preventing out-of-turn actions

F02_1_3.name: bid_history_tracker
F02_1_3.desc: Records the sequence of all bids made during the round, maintaining an audit trail of each player's decisions for use in determining outcomes and supporting UI display

F02_1_4.name: landlord_resolver
F02_1_4.desc: Applies the game's bidding rules to determine the final landlord winner once bidding concludes, handling tiebreakers, fallback logic when no one bids, and triggering a redeal if necessary

F02_1_5.name: bid_multiplier_calculator
F02_1_5.desc: Computes the stake multiplier based on bidding activity (e.g., how many players called vs grabbed), which affects the final scoring for the round

F06_1.name: play_analyzer
F06_1.desc: Analyzes the current play on the table to determine its type (single, pair, triple, sequence, etc.) and rank, establishing the constraints any response must satisfy

F06_2.name: valid_move_finder
F06_2.desc: Searches the AI's hand to enumerate all legal plays that can beat the current play, organizing candidates by strength to identify the smallest valid option

F06_3.name: bomb_evaluator
F06_3.desc: Evaluates whether deploying a bomb (or rocket) is strategically warranted based on game state, remaining cards, and urgency to win, overriding normal play hierarchy

F06_4.name: hand_decomposer
F06_4.desc: Splits the AI's hand into optimal groupings (singles, pairs, triples, sequences, etc.) to plan efficient card shedding and minimize leftover weak cards

F06_5.name: pass_decider
F06_5.desc: Determines when the AI should pass (not play), either because no valid play exists or because strategically conserving stronger cards is more advantageous

F04_1.name: card_pattern_classifier
F04_1.desc: Analyzes a set of played cards and classifies them into a specific pattern type (single, pair, triple, straight, consecutive pairs, airplane, bomb, rocket, etc.) based on card counts and rank sequences

F04_2.name: kicker_resolver
F04_2.desc: For patterns that allow kickers (triple+kicker, airplane+wings), identifies and validates the kicker cards, ensuring they meet the rules (e.g., correct number of kickers, kickers form valid sub-patterns)

F04_3.name: sequence_validator
F04_3.desc: Validates sequential/consecutive constraints for straights, consecutive pairs, and airplane patterns, checking that ranks are contiguous and meet minimum length requirements while excluding restricted ranks (e.g., 2, jokers)

F04_4.name: pattern_comparator
F04_4.desc: Compares two plays of the same pattern type by their leading rank to determine which is larger, and handles special override rules where bombs beat non-bombs and rockets beat everything

F02_2_1.name: bid_winner_resolver
F02_2_1.desc: Determines the winning bidder by evaluating the highest valid bid from the bidding round, resolving ties if multiple players bid the same amount

F02_2_2.name: default_landlord_selector
F02_2_2.desc: Selects a landlord randomly (or by predefined rule such as card-based selection) when no player has placed a bid during the bidding phase

F02_2_3.name: role_assignment_updater
F02_2_3.desc: Updates each player's role designation (landlord vs. peasant) in the player data structures once the landlord has been identified

F02_2_4.name: landlord_privilege_applicator
F02_2_4.desc: Grants landlord-specific privileges such as dealing the hidden bonus cards to the landlord's hand and adjusting scoring multipliers tied to the landlord role

F02_2_5.name: game_state_transition_emitter
F02_2_5.desc: Transitions the overall game state from the bidding/selection phase to the active play phase, broadcasting the landlord assignment to all players and logging the event

A.name: deck_cards
A.desc: The full array of 54 shuffled cards before and during dealing

B.name: player_hand
B.desc: Array of card objects currently held by the human player

C.name: ai_hands
C.desc: Arrays of card objects held by each of the two AI players

D.name: landlord_role
D.desc: Which player (human or AI-1 or AI-2) is the landlord, plus the 3 landlord bonus cards

E.name: selected_cards
E.desc: The subset of hand cards currently highlighted/selected by the human player via clicks

F.name: current_play
F.desc: The most recent valid card play on the table: cards, pattern type, rank, and who played it

G.name: pattern_result
G.desc: The identified pattern type and comparison outcome (valid/invalid, bigger/smaller) for a proposed play

H.name: active_turn
H.desc: Identifier of whose turn it currently is and the turn sequence state

I.name: ai_remaining_count
I.desc: Number of cards remaining in each AI player's hand (displayed without revealing faces)

J.name: hint_suggestion
J.desc: A recommended set of cards the player could play, shown as highlighted cards when hint button is pressed

K.name: game_result
K.desc: Win/loss outcome: which side won (landlord or farmers), triggers result dialog and restart option

L.name: button_actions
L.desc: Player UI actions: play, pass, hint button press events and their enabled/disabled states

schemas.C: { ai1: { suit: string; rank: string }[]; ai2: { suit: string; rank: string }[] }
schemas.D: { player: 'human' | 'ai1' | 'ai2'; bonusCards: { suit: string; rank: string }[] }
schemas.F: { cards: { suit: string; rank: string }[]; pattern: string; rank: number; player: 'human' | 'ai1' | 'ai2' } | null
schemas.G: { pattern: string; valid: boolean; comparison: 'bigger' | 'smaller' | 'equal' | null }
schemas.L: { action: 'play' | 'pass' | 'hint'; enabled: { play: boolean; pass: boolean; hint: boolean } }

F01.name: deck_manager
F01.desc: Handles shuffling 54 cards, dealing 17 cards to each player, assigning 3 landlord cards, and resetting for new games

F02.name: landlord_selector
F02.desc: Randomly determines which of the three players (human or AI) becomes the landlord and assigns the 3 bonus cards

F03.name: turn_controller
F03.desc: Manages counter-clockwise turn order, tracks whose turn it is, enforces pass logic, and detects round resets

F04.name: card_pattern_judge
F04.desc: Identifies card patterns (single, pair, triple+kicker, straight, consecutive pairs, airplane, bomb, rocket) and compares sizes

F05.name: player_input
F05.desc: Handles mouse click selection/deselection of hand cards, and play/pass/hint button clicks

F06.name: ai_engine
F06.desc: AI decision-making: finds smallest valid play, follows current play, splits hands or uses bombs when necessary, or passes

F07.name: win_detector
F07.desc: Checks after each play whether any player's hand is empty, determines win/loss result, triggers game-over state

F08.name: ui_renderer
F08.desc: Renders player hand area, play area, AI card counts, control buttons, status bar, highlight states, and result dialog
